skill.name: typescript-strict
skill.description: Guides TypeScript authors toward strict, type-safe patterns that reduce unsafe casts, implicit returns, and fragile error handling.
skill.summary: Enforces strict TypeScript habits for safer, clearer code.
skill.author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution, a YAML description key as weak cryptography, and TypeScript Array.find examples as reconnaissance. Review found no executable scripts, network access, filesystem access, credential handling, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Detected as Commands
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown headings, code fences, and inline TypeScript examples. They do not execute Ruby, shell commands, or external processes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file contains instructional Markdown and TypeScript snippets only. No shell command syntax, script runner, or executable wrapper is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Text Detected as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert points to the YAML description line, not to cryptographic code. No hashing, encryption, or crypto API usage appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata describing TypeScript usage. It contains no algorithm name, crypto implementation, or security-sensitive operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: TypeScript find Examples Detected as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts point to Array.find examples in TypeScript code guidance. They are not operating system discovery commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines demonstrate null checking around a TypeScript array lookup. There is no command execution, host inspection, or environment enumeration.

content.user_title: Write Strict TypeScript With Safer Types
content.value_statement: Loose TypeScript can hide runtime errors behind unsafe casts and implicit behavior. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward strict, type-safe code.
actual_capabilities: Discourages use of any and recommends unknown with type guards.
Requires explicit return types for function signatures.
Promotes safe error handling with unknown and Error checks.
Warns against unexplained non-null assertions and ts-ignore comments.
Recommends discriminated unions for typed success and failure states.
Encourages inference for obvious local values while keeping public APIs explicit.
limitations: Does not run the TypeScript compiler or inspect build output.
Does not provide project-specific tsconfig settings by itself.
Does not replace framework or domain-specific TypeScript standards.
Does not automatically migrate an existing codebase.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests for Type Safety
use_cases.description: Check changed TypeScript files for unsafe any usage, missing return types, and fragile assertions.

use_cases.title: Draft New Strict TypeScript Modules
use_cases.description: Create new services, utilities, or handlers that start with explicit types and safe error handling.

use_cases.title: Teach Consistent TypeScript Habits
use_cases.description: Use concise examples to explain safer alternatives to assertions, casts, and weak result shapes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Type Safety Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small TypeScript change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this TypeScript change using strict TypeScript rules. Focus on any usage, missing return types, and unsafe assertions.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite Unsafe Function
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve one function without changing behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this function to avoid any, add an explicit return type, and handle unknown errors safely.

prompt_templates.title: Model Result States
prompt_templates.scenario: Design safer data flow for success and failure cases.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this result handling into a discriminated union and explain how each branch becomes type-safe.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Module for Strictness
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across a full TypeScript module.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this module for strict TypeScript compliance. Identify risky casts, implicit returns, ts-ignore comments, and opportunities for discriminated unions.

output_examples.input: A service function accepts any input and returns different shapes for errors and success.
output_examples.output: The review flags the unsafe input type, recommends unknown with validation, and suggests a clear success or failure result shape.

output_examples.input: A pull request adds non-null assertions after array lookups.
output_examples.output: The review explains the runtime risk and recommends explicit missing-value handling before using the selected item.

output_examples.input: A catch block assumes every thrown value has a message property.
output_examples.output: The review recommends treating the caught value as unknown and narrowing it before logging or returning error details.

best_practices: Ask for focused reviews on one module or pull request at a time.
Share project conventions when they differ from the default strict guidance.
Use the recommendations together with compiler checks and tests.
anti_patterns: Using this skill as a substitute for running TypeScript validation.
Requesting broad rewrites without explaining behavior that must stay stable.
Applying every suggestion without checking project constraints.

faq.question: Does this skill run TypeScript checks?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance for strict TypeScript style and review. Use your compiler and tests for verification.

faq.question: Can it help remove any from existing code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest unknown, type guards, and stronger interfaces for safer replacements.

faq.question: Does it forbid all type assertions?
faq.answer: No. It asks the assistant to minimize assertions and explain them when they are necessary.

faq.question: Is this useful for new projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps establish strict habits before unsafe patterns spread through the codebase.

faq.question: Can teams customize the guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide your local standards, framework rules, and tsconfig expectations in the prompt.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists all three tools as supported targets.
